name: What's Your Creative Thinking Style?
description: Discover how your mind naturally approaches creative challenges and problem-solving

innovator.title: The Boundary Breaker
innovator.description: You excel at radical innovation and thinking outside conventional frameworks. Your creativity shines when challenging existing paradigms and developing completely original concepts that change how people see the world.
characteristics: Seeks to transform rather than improve
innovator.challenges: May struggle with practical implementation or incremental progress

synthesizer.title: The Connection Maker
synthesizer.description: Your creativity flows from combining existing ideas in novel ways. You spot patterns and relationships others miss, creating value through unexpected combinations and interdisciplinary thinking.
synthesizer.strengths: Integration, interdisciplinary thinking, pattern recognition, adaptability
synthesizer.challenges: May sometimes force connections that aren't meaningful

refiner.title: The Master Craftsman
refiner.description: Your creative genius lies in perfecting and improving existing ideas. You take good concepts and elevate them through meticulous attention to detail, execution quality, and incremental enhancements.
refiner.strengths: Execution, craftsmanship, attention to detail, quality focus
refiner.challenges: May hesitate to share unfinished work or pursue radical changes

improviser.title: The Spontaneous Creator
improviser.description: You thrive on immediacy and spontaneity in your creative process. Your best ideas emerge in the moment, responding to immediate constraints and opportunities with flexible, adaptive thinking.
improviser.strengths: Adaptability, spontaneity, performance under pressure, quick thinking
improviser.challenges: May struggle with long-term projects requiring sustained focus

questions.question: When starting a creative project, you typically:
options: Challenge all the basic assumptions first
Look for inspiration from unrelated fields
Study excellent examples to understand standards
Dive right in and figure it out as you go

questions.question: Your ideal creative environment is:
options: Somewhere with no rules or limitations
Surrounded by diverse stimuli and references
A well-organized, high-quality workspace
Anywhere - you can create in chaotic situations

questions.question: When faced with a complex problem, what is your first step?
options: Brainstorm innovative ideas without boundaries
Connect different concepts to form a cohesive solution
Analyze the details and meticulously plan a solution
Experiment with various approaches spontaneously

questions.question: How do you typically generate new ideas?
options: I often think outside the box and challenge the status quo
I blend concepts from various sources to create something new
I focus on improving and perfecting existing ideas
I trust my instincts and improvise solutions in the moment

questions.question: What best describes your creative process?
options: I start with a burst of original, unconventional ideas
I gather diverse inputs and merge them into a unified vision
I focus on refining every detail to perfect the idea
I dive in, adapting and modifying my approach as I go

questions.question: When planning a project, which approach resonates with you most?
options: Innovating from scratch with a fresh perspective
Synthesizing various ideas into a coherent plan
Meticulously refining every aspect before starting
Jumping into action and adapting as challenges arise

questions.question: How do you tackle unexpected challenges in a project?
options: By generating unconventional solutions on the spot
By integrating unforeseen ideas into my plan
By methodically analyzing and refining my strategy
By improvising quickly to adapt to the situation

questions.question: Which statement best reflects your view on creativity?
options: Creativity is about breaking new ground and exploring the unknown
It’s about connecting disparate ideas into a unified whole
It involves refining concepts until they are perfectly realized
It thrives on spontaneity and the freedom to experiment

questions.question: How do you approach brainstorming sessions?
options: I lead with radical, groundbreaking concepts
I facilitate the merging of various ideas
I concentrate on perfecting details through careful thought
I let ideas flow freely and adjust them as we discuss
